figures.caption: Figure 2: Example task walkthrough: multi-rebalance-on-call-rotation. The agent reads from Docs, queries Calendar, reviews Slack history, and posts an update. The evaluator compares preand post-execution database states to assign a score. This task is one of only two never-solved tasks in the benchmark: no model solves it in any of the 33 conditions (Section 5).
Table 22: Sandbox escalation tool-call counts across all conditions, by escalation category. Counts are total tool calls (not trajectories) exhibiting each pattern.
Table 9: Safety-relevant architectural differences between OpenClaw and Gemini CLI.

Table 10: Experimental coverage matrix. Each cell indicates whether a condition was run (✓) or not. Five combinations have a full 2× 2 factorial; five have only corner conditions; one has three cells.
Figure 8: Slack UI used in the environment.
Table 24: Unauthorized contract modification rates on multi-unapproved-clause-deploy (all scaffolding conditions pooled). “All 5” = agent modified all 5 client contracts.

Table 1: Comparison with most related benchmarks. Svc = number of distinct service APIs. Stateful = controllable mock environment with database-backed state. Safety = separated safety/refusal evaluation. Skills = supports structured skill specifications that can be varied independently. — denotes not reported or not applicable.
Table 17: Safety-violation rates (% of trials with negative scores) by harness/model and condition. Columns denote skills-on/off × meta-on/off conditions.
Figure 6: Google Doc UI used in the environment.
Figure 7: Google Email UI used in the environment.

Table 4: Model comparison on OpenClaw at unscaffolded (off/off) and fully scaffolded (on/on) conditions. Sk = Domain Skills; Mt = Meta Prompt. TSR = Task Success Rate (non-safety, ≥ 0.8). UAR = Unsafe Action Rate (safety, < 0). SCR = Safe Completion Rate (safety, ≥ 0.8). Task-level cluster bootstrap 95% CIs in brackets. Full 33-condition results in Table 11 (Appendix).
Table 19: Effort statistics at off/off and on/on conditions. Dur. = mean duration in seconds. TC = mean tool calls. TO = timeout rate.
Figure 4: Google Calendar UI used in the environment.
Figure 5: Google Drive UI used in the environment.

Table 7: Comparison of publicly available agentic benchmarks across major evaluation categories. DB Tables reports the number of unique database tables in the environment; Avg. FK measures average foreign keys per table, indicating relational density. — denotes values not reported in the original work. ∗Avg. Steps reflects ideal human-authored execution trajectories. †Parenthetical values indicate the number of unique task templates.

Table 21: Infrastructure probing rates at baseline (sks-off / meta-off). A trajectory is counted as probing if it contains ≥1 of: curl to localhost, env/printenv, openapi.json fetch, credential file search, or harness binary inspection.

Table 11: Full results across all 33 conditions, grouped by harness. Sk = Domain Skills; Mt = Meta Prompt. TSR = Task Success Rate (non-safety, ≥ 0.8). UAR = Unsafe Action Rate (safety, < 0). SCR = Safe Completion Rate (safety, ≥ 0.8). Task-level cluster bootstrap 95% CIs in brackets.
